events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need evaluate proposed recovery plan. We cannot see image beyond description, but in scenario text: "PROPOSED RECOVERY: To recover 2 day(s), the foreman proposes to hang drywall while the insulation inspection is still pending (start L before K com...)" The legend says red=inspection hold cannot bypass. Activity K is insulation inspection [1d] <- J. L Drywall hang [3d] <- K. So proposed to start L before K completed, bypassing inspection hold. That violates hard constraint inspection hold cannot bypass. Decision fail. Need also work_stage in-progress. component_conditions non-compliant maybe because schedule plan is non-compliant? Task says categories may apply: non-compliant. So include non-compliant. percent_complete estimate overall progress? At observed stage in-progress, maybe ~65%? Need derive from schedule. But we can approximate based on early-start schedule length 35 days, and current day? Not given. The task asks numeric estimate of overall progress. We could estimate from Gantt maybe a vertical line? Image not showing current date. But scenario says stage in-progress (actively in progress). We need a value. Maybe 50%. Let's reason.

Schedule: A (0-2), B(2-4), C(4-5), CC cure (5-8), D(8-13), E(13-15), F(15-19), G(15-17), H(15-20), I inspection (20-21), J(21-23), K inspection (23-24), L(24-27), M(27-30), N(30-33), O(33-35), P(33-34), Q final (35-36). Total about 36 days. If in-progress maybe around after insulation inspection? Not sure. But plan under review includes drywall not yet started. We can estimate percent complete maybe around 65% (24/36). Since L is about to start, activities A-K complete = up to day 24, total 36 => 66.7%. That seems plausible. Use percent_complete 67.

Workable activities? The task asks use workable for list of activity IDs when task asks what work can still start. The task asks decide what work can proceed, what is blocked, and what recovery plan is valid. So provide workable: activities not dependent on K pending? Currently with K pending, L blocked. Work that can proceed? J? J is predecessor to K; K is inspection maybe not started or pending. If insulation inspection is pending, drywall hang cannot proceed. Other future activities M,N,O,P,Q are blocked by L or each other. But maybe after K completes, L,M,N,O,P,Q can proceed. In current state (K pending), only inspection K itself can proceed/work? But it's an inspection hold. Could insulation work J be complete? If K is pending, J done. No work can start until K complete except K inspection itself. Maybe also activities not in that chain? All subsequent are dependent. So workable empty? But need maybe include K as activity that must be completed; but "workable" means can still start. The question asks what work can proceed, what is blocked. In answer we can put workable: [] because L is blocked until K is passed; all downstream blocked. However maybe if K is pending, you can start K (inspection) but it's an inspection hold not work. Hmm.

Findings: proposed recovery bypasses inspection hold; drywall hang started before insulation inspection; violates hard constraint; inspection holds cannot be bypassed; creates risk of covering non-compliant/uncertified insulation, concealed work without sign-off, code violation, potential rework.

Actions: reject recovery plan; maintain sequence; complete insulation inspection K before starting drywall L; revise schedule to recover time elsewhere (e.g., compress non-critical activities with float? Maybe there is no float on critical path; could add resources to D,H,F? But hard constraints can't compress cure lags/inspections). Could suggest overlap M and N? Actually M->N FS. Could add crew to reduce durations? Not asked. Action: immediate corrective action: stop/start? Do not start L until K passed; enforce inspection hold.

Risk high or critical? Bypassing inspection hold is non-compliant and could result in concealed defects, rework, code enforcement, safety. Decision fail b…
